author: chfast
content: e.g. for pre-funded accounts. We pay NEW_ACCOUNT up front although it already exists and the refund this at the very end 🙂

author: lu_pinto
content: thought we already charged access to the callee account so you can check if it is prefunded before you charge `NEW_ACCOUNT`

author: chfast
content: I'd need to double check then. So far It looks I'm not passing v6.1.0 if I change this.

author: chfast
content: The strange part: we check `target_alive` before a child frame, and after the frame we refund if `True`: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/forks/amsterdam/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/vm/instructions/system.py#L166-L167

author: chfast
content:
This is the change by <@968526635735326741>, but I'm not sure the problem is fully solved. The implementation is actually doing the account check anyway before the frame. Does it mean this check is not recorded in the BAL? I.e. EELS does the checks in the prev order, just applies gas in different order 🙄

https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11807

author: chfast
content:
I partly understand the motivation, but still I don't understand why not fix this properly:
- charge base gas cost
- check depth,
- check balance,
- check if create target exists, if not apply NEW_ACCOUNT cost.
